title: Boat Rental in Mombasa: Top Things to Do by Boat in 2026
html:
Mombasa offers a unique gateway to the Indian Ocean, where renting a boat lets you explore coral reefs, historic creeks, and sandy islands. Whether you choose a dhow from Nyali Beach or a motor yacht from the Likoni Ferry channel, expect prices from EUR 150 for a half-day trip. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare verified boat listings from local operators.

Where to set off in Mombasa?

Start your boating adventure from Nyali Beach, where several operators offer day trips. Other key departure points include Mombasa Yacht Club in the old harbour, the Likoni Ferry slipway for south-coast access, Mtwapa Creek for mangrove tours, Wasini Island for dolphin spotting, and Diani Beach (a 30-minute drive south) for reef snorkelling. Each location suits different boat types and itineraries.

Which boat to rent in Mombasa?

  1. Sailing dhow, best for sunset cruises in Mtwapa Creek, up to 12 guests, from EUR 30 per person.
  2. Small motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for fishing or island hopping, up to 8 guests, from EUR 250 for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), perfect for family day trips to Shimoni Caves, up to 14 guests, from EUR 650 full-day.
  4. Motor yacht (e.g. Fairline Targa 43), offers a luxury experience with cabins, up to 10 guests, from EUR 850 full-day. See the Fairline Targa 43 for hire on BoatHire24 for details.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mombasa?

Prices vary by boat and duration. Shared tours start at EUR 50 per person for a half-day dhow trip. For private charters, expect these bands:

  • Shared dhow tour: EUR 50, 70 per person for 3, 4 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day: EUR 250, 350 for up to 8 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day: EUR 650, 950 for up to 14 guests.
  • Crewed yacht (like the Sunseeker 50): EUR 1,200, 1,800 full-day for up to 12 guests.

You can also compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to find the best deal for your group.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mombasa?

No licence is required for skippered charters; bareboat rentals may require proof of competence, which your operator confirms. Most visitors choose a captain-included option, which is safer and more relaxing. For self-drive dinghies, a brief orientation suffices. Always check with the operator before booking.

For dolphin watching, the best months are January to March when the sea is calm and the north-east monsoon (Kaskazi) blows. Head to Wasini Channel early morning for the highest sightings.

At a glance: Mombasa boat rental

  • Price range: EUR 50 (shared dhow) to EUR 1,800 (luxury yacht)
  • Peak season: December, March and July, August
  • Typical trip: 3, 8 hours
  • Group capacity: 1, 14 guests
  • Currency: EUR (quoted for ease; some operators accept USD)

Questions about boat rental in Mombasa

Can I rent a boat for a day from Mombasa?

Yes, many operators offer day trips from EUR 250 for a half-day, including lunch and snorkelling gear.

Is it safe to take children on a boat trip in Mombasa?

Yes, skippered boats provide life jackets and briefings; choose a calm-water creek or reef sheltered from the open ocean.

What should I bring on a boat rental in Mombasa?

Bring sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, a towel, and cash for optional tips or park fees; operators supply drinks and snacks on full-day trips.

How far is Wasini Island from Mombasa by boat?

Wasini Island is about 35 nautical miles south of Mombasa, a 45-minute trip by speedboat; a catamaran takes around 1.5 hours.

For practical booking advice, refer to the booking FAQ on BoatHire24.

Season and tips for Mombasa boating

The best weather runs from January to March (Kaskazi winds) and July to August (south-east monsoon, Kusi), offering flat seas and blue skies. April, June and October, November bring rain and stronger winds, making some routes less enjoyable. A tip: negotiate a price that includes fuel; many charters quote without it.
